name: Chicken Potpies
recipeIngredient: 1 recipe Pastry for Double-Crust Pie, see next recipe
3/4 teaspoon dried sage, marjoram, or thyme, crushed
3 cups cubed cooked chicken or turkey (1 pound)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare Pastry for Double-Crust Pie to make 6 individual casseroles or Pastry for Single-Crust Pie to make a 2-quart casserole; set aside.
2. Cook frozen vegetables according to package directions; drain.
3. In a medium saucepan, cook onion and mushrooms in hot margarine or butter until tender.
4. Stir in flour, sage, thyme, salt, and pepper.
5. Add broth and 3/4 cup milk all at once.
6. Cook and stir until thickened and bubbly.
7. Pour chicken mixture into six (10-oz.) casseroles or a 2-quart round casserole.
8. For the 6 individual casseroles, roll double-crust pastry to a 15x10-inch rectangle; cut into six 5-inch circles.
9. For the 2-quart casserole, roll single-crust pastry into a 10-inch circle.
10. Place crusts on top of casseroles, flute edges.
11. Brush with milk; top with cutouts, if using.
12. Sprinkle with sesame seed, if desired.
13. Bake in a 450°F oven until pastry is golden brown (allowing 12 to 15 minutes for the large casserole).
14. Makes 6 servings.
15. Biscuit-Topped Chicken Potpie: Prepare as above, except omit pastry. Cut dough rounds from 1 package (6) refrigerated biscuits into quarters; arrange atop bubbly chicken mixture in casserole(s). Bake in a 400°F oven for about 15 minutes or until biscuits are golden.
